learningObjective: See how the derivative is defined as the limit of the slope of a secant line as two points converge
ahaMoment: Watching the secant line tilt and become the tangent as h approaches zero

miniSpec.title: Difference Quotient Table
miniSpec.primary_visual_feedback: Secant line tilts toward tangent line, table shows slope converging to derivative
miniSpec.causal_chain_exposed: Drag point closer → h decreases → slope updates → approaches 2a → secant becomes tangent
miniSpec.aha_moment: As h→0, the secant slope converges to exactly 2a (the derivative)

modelVariables.derivativeH: the distance h between the two points (0.01-3)
modelVariables.derivativeA: the base x-value where we calculate the derivative
modelVariables.secantSlope: derived: the current slope of the secant line

assertions.description: On load, h is large (2) so secant differs visibly from tangent — the gap invites exploration
assertions.visualCheck: Orange secant line clearly tilted differently from green dashed tangent

assertions.description: Reducing h causes the secant slope to approach the derivative
assertions.visualCheck: Secant line tilts closer to tangent, table shows slope approaching 2

assertions.description: At very small h, secant slope nearly equals the derivative
assertions.visualCheck: Secant line nearly overlaps tangent, slope in table very close to 2
